board.designation: DB-GENSET-XCV — Standby-genset changeover sub-DB with ATS (utility-priority), IEC commercial 3-storey office
board.location: external genset enclosure with ATS in ground-floor plant room

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSB-MAIN F08 (63A MCCB Type D, 60m feeder) AND standby genset 80 kVA via ATS (utility-priority)

main_switch._note: Integrated with ATS — utility/genset transfer via mechanical interlock per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 (4-pole transfer, neutral switched)

circuits.designation: EM lighting changeover feed to DB-EM
Fire alarm changeover feed to DB-FA1
UPS bypass mains backup to DB-UPS

selectivity_results.upstream: MSB-MAIN F08 (63A MCCB Type D, utility-side) → ATS → DB-GENSET-XCV main switch
selectivity_results.downstream: DB-GENSET-XCV main switch + 6A-32A Type C MCBs on ATS-output side
selectivity_results._note: 63A MCCB Type D upstream cascading via the ATS to 6-32A Type C MCBs at DB-GENSET-XCV. MCCB-upstream-of-MCB cascade benefits from device-class differential (Type D magnetic trip 10-20×In vs Type C 5-10×In) — IEC 60898 + IEC 60947-2 cascade tables typically grant time-current selectivity at ratios ≥1.5:1 across these classes. Worst-case ratio at G02/G04 (32A) = 63:32 = ~2:1 — at the IEC 60898 same-curve cascade threshold and ABOVE the MCCB-class cross-discrimination threshold. Dual-mode fault contribution: utility-side declared PFC 9.0 kA (IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5 utility worst case); genset-mode subtransient Ik" ≈ 1.0 kA on the 80 kVA standby genset per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5.1 equivalent voltage source method (In ≈ 115.5 A, Xd" ≈ 0.12 pu per IEC 60909-1:2002 Table A.1, E" ≈ 1.05 pu → Ik" ≈ 115.5 × 1.05/0.12 ≈ 1.01 kA; peak ip ≈ 2.57 kA at κ ≈ 1.8; sustained Ik ≈ 0.4 kA after AVR field forcing settles ~5 s post-fault, which is what the 3-5× In figure references — NOT subtransient Ik") — significantly lower than utility-mode. Flagged INFO for fault-level skill to verify against manufacturer cascade table at BOTH 9.0 kA utility-mode + ~1.0 kA genset-mode subtransient Ik" (sustained Ik ~0.4 kA after AVR settles), including ATS transfer-time impact on cascade behaviour.

non_compliance_flags.message: ATS transfer time requirement — typical 6-10 s for a medium standby genset (engine crank + warm-up + breaker close + load step) PER ISO 8528-12:1997. Engineer to confirm AHJ acceptance against the strictest applicable cross-jurisdictional benchmark: BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 (central-battery emergency-lighting transfer time ≤0.5 s if classified as Type Z, else ≤15 s for Type N — DB-EM downstream of G02 must specify), NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 (≤10 s for life-safety branch — US-aligned facilities), and IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §556+§551 (safety services + LV generating sets — engineer-declared per AHJ). The 6-10 s genset-only transfer is INSUFFICIENT for Type Z emergency lighting (≤0.5 s) — those circuits MUST be backed by central-battery EM lighting or local-battery luminaires per BS EN 50171:2001 §6.3, and G02 here merely provides the post-genset feed once the ATS has completed transfer.

assumptions: DB-GENSET-XCV is the dedicated standby-genset changeover sub-DB — `role: ats_changeover_panel` flagged in the board block; supply_class: essential signals to downstream consumers (riser, generator-sizing, fault-level, SLD) that this distribution feeds safety-services and essential life-safety loads with dual-source resilience.

IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 governs the ATS (automatic transfer switching) installation: 4-pole transfer (L1+L2+L3+N) so the genset neutral does not back-feed the utility neutral during genset-mode operation. Mechanical AND electrical interlock between utility-source and genset-source contactors prevents parallel-source closure. Utility-priority logic: ATS rests on utility-source; on utility loss (undervoltage/phase-loss/frequency deviation sensed at ATS controller), ATS sends start signal to genset, waits for genset stable (≥95% V + ≥98% f for ≥3 s), opens utility contactor, dwells (typical 0.5-1 s), closes genset contactor, transfers load. On utility return + stable (≥95% V + ≥98% f for ≥30 s), reverses: opens genset, dwells, closes utility, sends genset-cooldown signal (≥5 min run unloaded), then stop.

ISO 8528-12:1997 governs the standby genset itself — emergency power supply to safety services. 80 kVA prime-rated diesel with electronic governor (frequency stability ≤1% per §6.3.2) + AVR (voltage stability ≤2.5% per §6.3.1). Type-tested per ISO 8528-5:2018 performance class G3 (industrial standard for non-PMG load steps).

Transfer time 6-10 s typical for an 80 kVA medium-genset on the utility-loss → genset-online sequence: 1-2 s utility-loss detection + 3-5 s engine crank-to-stable + 0.5-1 s ATS dwell + 0.5-1 s breaker close + 0.5-1 s load-step settling. ISO 8528-12:1997 classifies this as Class A1 (1 second) NOT achievable without a no-break/short-break system (e.g., flywheel UPS or rotary UPS) — this design relies on EM lighting central-battery + UPS bypass to ride through the 6-10 s window.

NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 cross-reference — US-aligned facilities require ≤10 s transfer for life-safety branch loads. The 6-10 s design intent sits at the boundary of Type 10 acceptance; AHJ to confirm against latest commissioning test report. EU facilities using BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 face stricter rules: Type Z emergency lighting requires ≤0.5 s transfer (only achievable with central-battery EM lighting or self-contained luminaires).

BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 transfer-time disclosure — G02 EM lighting feed to DB-EM does NOT itself meet the ≤0.5 s Type Z transfer time. The DB-EM downstream sub-DB must be a central-battery EM system (DB-EM in the peer dbem example uses a 24 kVA EM-CB unit with 3-hour autonomy) OR rely on self-contained luminaires per BS 5266-1:2016 §8.2.7 — both topologies handle the 6-10 s genset-transfer gap by running on battery during the transition. G02 here simply re-energises the EM-CB charger and the central inverter once the genset is online.

Declared PFC 9.0 kA at the ATS output represents the utility-mode worst case per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5 — derived from upstream MSB-MAIN F08 declared 22.5 kA reduced by the 60m feeder impedance (R+jX ≈ 0.27Ω at 16mm² Cu XLPE). Genset-mode subtransient Ik" is significantly lower per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5.1 equivalent voltage source method: In = 80,000 / (√3 × 400) ≈ 115.5 A; Xd" ≈ 0.12 pu typical for a 4-pole salient-pole synchronous machine per IEC 60909-1:2002 Table A.1; E" ≈ 1.05 pu; Ik" = In × E"/Xd" ≈ 115.5 × (1.05/0.12) ≈ 1.01 kA; peak ip ≈ κ × √2 × Ik" ≈ 2.57 kA at κ ≈ 1.8. Sustained Ik (after AVR field forcing settles ~5 s post-fault) ≈ 0.4 kA (3-5× In) — this is the figure sometimes mistakenly quoted as subtransient. Both regimes (utility-mode 9.0 kA + genset-mode subtransient Ik" ~1.0 kA + sustained Ik ~0.4 kA) must be flagged to the fault-level skill for full coordination study.

Genset-mode neutral bonding: per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552, neutral is switched at the ATS (4-pole transfer); a separate neutral-earth bonding contact closes on the genset side when the ATS transfers to genset-source, ensuring the genset operates as a separately derived TN-S system without parallel-neutral fault paths to the utility transformer.

G01 ATS controller is on the ELV_control voltage class — 24 VDC supervisory supply for the ATS logic + signaling (genset start/stop relay coil, status LEDs, transfer-event annunciator). 1.5mm² 3-core LSZH cable handles the small SMPS load (≤0.5 kW); not RCD-protected because it is a dedicated supervisory control circuit (not a socket circuit) per the same exemption principle as DB-UPS U05 BMS controller.

G02, G03, G04 (EM lighting + fire alarm + UPS bypass) are all RCD-FREE by deliberate design — these are life-safety supply paths per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 (NO RCD on safety-services emergency feeds) + IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4 (UPS bypass must allow upstream fault flow). An RCD on any of these would defeat the dual-source resilience purpose: under a downstream fault, the RCD would trip before the upstream MCCB/MCB could clear, losing the emergency feed at the worst possible moment.

G05 day tank + cooling fan is the ONLY RCD-protected circuit — Type A 30 mA per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.3 because it is a mechanical-services circuit feeding (a) a small fuel-handling pump motor (day tank fill from bulk tank) and (b) an exterior-routed cooling fan motor in the genset enclosure. Exterior-route + fuel-handling + motor windings together justify additional shock protection beyond basic-insulation reliance. Type A handles pulsating-DC leakage from the small Class 1 single-phase motors.

All Type C MCBs (Ia = 10×In) on the ATS-output side — handles motor inrush at G05, lighting-driver inrush at G02 (SMPS-fed EM lighting central battery rectifier), fire-alarm-panel SMPS inrush at G03, and UPS rectifier inrush at G04. Type C with 10 kA Icn covers the 9.0 kA utility-mode declared PFC at the ATS output.

Upstream MSB-MAIN F08 is a 63A MCCB Type D — Type D selected for the 60m feeder run because the higher magnetic-trip threshold (10-20×In) tolerates the genset start-up transient that travels back upstream through the ATS during transfer (genset block-load step at the moment the ATS closes the genset contactor produces a brief overcurrent on the feeder as the genset stabilises). Type C upstream would risk nuisance trip during transfer.

Selectivity flagged as INFO (MCCB-upstream-of-MCB cascade) — 63:32 ratio at G02/G04 = ~2:1 sits AT the IEC 60898 same-curve minimum BUT benefits from MCCB-class cross-discrimination (Type D magnetic trip 10-20×In vs Type C 5-10×In) per IEC 60947-2 cascade table. Fault-level skill must verify against manufacturer cascade table at both 9.0 kA utility-mode AND ~1.0 kA genset-mode subtransient Ik" (sustained Ik ~0.4 kA after AVR settles) + ATS transfer-time impact on cascade behaviour.

ATS transfer-time disclosure carries warning severity (not info) because the 6-10 s design intent is at the boundary of NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 acceptance + below BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 Type Z threshold. AHJ confirmation required before commissioning — this is a hard sign-off gate, not a future check.

drawing_notes: DB-GENSET-XCV located with the ATS section in the ground-floor plant room (IP4X indoor) and the genset/contactor enclosure section co-located with the 80 kVA standby genset in the external genset enclosure (IP54 weather-rated). Short tie cables between the two sections through a sealed wall penetration per IEC 60364-5-52 §522.8.3 fire-stopping.

Fed from MSB-MAIN F08 (63A MCCB Type D) via 60m 16mm² 4-core Cu XLPE submain to the ATS utility-source input. ATS genset-source input fed from 80 kVA standby genset via 25mm² 4-core Cu XLPE (sized for genset full-load + start transient + 4-pole transfer requirement).

Utility-priority ATS logic per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552: rests on utility-source; on utility loss, starts genset and transfers after stability achieved; on utility return + 30 s stable, reverses to utility and runs genset unloaded for 5-min cooldown before stop.

Transfer time: 6-10 s typical (ISO 8528-12:1997 medium-genset) — NOT compatible with BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 Type Z emergency lighting (≤0.5 s); downstream DB-EM is a central-battery EM system bridging the gap.

5 circuits + 3 spare ways (W6-W8 reserved — typical future expansion: additional safety-services feed (CCTV control, security panel), additional mechanical-services exterior load (block heater for genset cold-climate retrofit), or genset-room utility lighting/socket.
Role: ats_changeover_panel — flagged in board block for downstream SLD/riser/fault-level/generator-sizing/earthing consumers; supply_class: essential signals safety-services dual-source resilience.
G01 ATS controller on ELV_control voltage class (24 VDC supervisory); G02/G03/G04 on LV_power (4-pole life-safety feeds); G05 on LV_power (mechanical-services 1-phase + N + E).

Type A RCD on G05 ONLY (day tank + cooling fan, exterior-routed motor circuit) per §411.3.3. G01 (control), G02 (EM lighting feed), G03 (fire alarm feed), G04 (UPS bypass) are ALL RCD-free per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 + IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4 life-safety + bypass exemptions.
ALL Type C MCBs on ATS-output side; upstream MSB-MAIN F08 is Type D MCCB (handles genset block-load transfer transient).

4-pole transfer ATS (utility neutral switched separately from genset neutral) + genset-side neutral-earth bonding contact per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 — genset operates as separately derived TN-S system in genset-mode.

Dual-mode fault levels: 9.0 kA utility-mode (IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5 worst case after 60m feeder impedance) + ~1.0 kA genset-mode subtransient Ik" (IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5.1 equivalent voltage source on 80 kVA, In ≈ 115.5 A, Xd" ≈ 0.12 pu, E" ≈ 1.05 pu → Ik" ≈ 1.01 kA, peak ip ≈ 2.57 kA at κ ≈ 1.8; sustained Ik ~0.4 kA after AVR settles ~5 s post-fault). Both modes flagged for fault-level skill.

Placard on DB-GENSET-XCV faceplate: 'DUAL-SOURCE — UTILITY/GENSET ATS — DO NOT BACK-FEED' per IEEE 446-1995 critical-power labelling practice + IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 dual-source warning requirement.

WARNING flag: ATS transfer time (6-10 s) is AT the NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 boundary and BELOW BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 Type Z threshold — AHJ sign-off required at commissioning; downstream EM lighting must be central-battery or self-contained to bridge the gap.

rationale.chat_summary: DB-GENSET-XCV: 63A TPN ATS changeover sub-DB (utility-priority, 80 kVA standby genset, IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 4-pole transfer). 5 circuits: G01 ATS-controller ELV + G02 EM-lighting + G03 fire-alarm + G04 UPS-bypass (all RCD-free life-safety per §560.7 + IEC 62040-1 §5.2.4) + G05 day-tank/fan Type A 30mA. WARNING: 6-10s transfer (ISO 8528-12:1997) at NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 boundary, below BS EN 50171 §6.3 Type Z — AHJ sign-off required; downstream DB-EM bridges via central battery.

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: DB-GENSET-XCV is the dedicated standby-genset changeover sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, with the ATS section in the ground-floor plant room (IP4X) and the contactor-enclosure section co-located with the 80 kVA external standby genset (IP54). Role flagged as `ats_changeover_panel` and supply_class as `essential` — signals dual-source resilience to downstream SLD, riser, fault-level, and generator-sizing skills so they treat this distribution as a safety-services bus with utility/genset alternative-source coordination and dedicated ATS-transfer-time disclosure.

decisions.label: Board role flagged as ats_changeover_panel
decisions.summary: Signals dual-source ATS-fed distribution to all downstream skills (SLD, riser, fault-level, generator-sizing, earthing).

decisions.label: supply_class: essential
decisions.summary: Safety-services bus — essential life-safety loads (EM lighting + fire alarm + UPS bypass) with dual-source resilience.

decisions.label: Split-location enclosure (ATS indoor + contactor outdoor)
decisions.summary: ATS controller + utility-source breaker in ground-floor plant room (IP4X); genset-source contactor + transfer cabling in external IP54 genset enclosure; sealed wall penetration per IEC 60364-5-52 §522.8.3.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 63A TPN intake fed from MSB-MAIN F08 (63A MCCB Type D) via 60m 16mm² Cu XLPE submain to the ATS utility-source input, AND from an 80 kVA standby genset via 25mm² Cu XLPE to the ATS genset-source input. Utility-priority logic per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552: rests on utility, auto-starts genset on utility loss, transfers when genset stable (≥95% V + ≥98% f for ≥3 s), reverses on utility return + 30 s stable. Transfer time 6-10 s typical for an 80 kVA medium-genset per ISO 8528-12:1997 — DOES NOT meet BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 Type Z (≤0.5 s) and sits AT the NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 (≤10 s) boundary.

decisions.label: Supply class: essential
decisions.summary: Safety-services bus with dual-source resilience — utility + genset via ATS.

decisions.label: 4-pole transfer ATS (neutral switched)
decisions.summary: Genset operates as separately derived TN-S system in genset-mode; prevents parallel-neutral fault paths to utility transformer.

decisions.label: Utility-priority logic
decisions.summary: Default rest on utility; auto-start genset on utility loss; reverse-transfer on utility return + 30 s stable + 5-min cooldown.

decisions.label: Upstream MSB-MAIN F08 = Type D MCCB
decisions.summary: Type D magnetic trip 10-20×In tolerates genset block-load transfer transient travelling upstream through ATS; Type C would risk nuisance trip during transfer.

decisions.label: Dual-mode declared PFC: 9.0 kA utility / ~1.0 kA genset subtransient Ik" (sustained Ik ~0.4 kA)
decisions.summary: Utility-mode worst case per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.5 (after 60m feeder impedance); genset-mode subtransient Ik" ≈ 1.01 kA per §3.5.1 equivalent voltage source on 80 kVA (In ≈ 115.5 A, Xd" ≈ 0.12 pu, E" ≈ 1.05 pu; peak ip ≈ 2.57 kA, κ ≈ 1.8). Sustained Ik ≈ 0.4 kA after AVR field forcing settles ~5 s post-fault.

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 5 final circuits: G01 ATS controller (6A ELV_control supervisory) + G02 EM lighting changeover feed to DB-EM (32A 3-phase) + G03 fire alarm changeover feed to DB-FA1 (20A 3-phase) + G04 UPS bypass mains backup to DB-UPS (32A 3-phase) + G05 genset day tank + cooling fan (10A 1-phase mechanical). The 4 downstream emergency feeds (G02-G04) re-energise their respective central-battery / battery-backed sub-DBs once the genset is online, bridging the 6-10 s transfer gap through their own battery autonomy.

decisions.label: G01 on ELV_control voltage class
decisions.summary: 24 VDC supervisory ATS controller + signaling — dedicated control circuit, not a socket circuit.

decisions.label: G02/G03/G04 on LV_power voltage class (4-pole life-safety feeds)
decisions.summary: Standard LV TPN distribution treatment; each feed re-energises a downstream battery-backed sub-DB once genset is online.
decisions.rule: voltage_class taxonomy for LV power distribution

decisions.label: G05 mechanical-services 1-phase + N + E
decisions.summary: Day tank fuel pump motor + cooling fan motor in exterior genset enclosure.

decisions.label: Sizing: 32A on G02/G04, 20A on G03, 10A on G05, 6A on G01
decisions.summary: Sized for downstream sub-DB intake capacity + voltage-drop margin across long 40-80m runs (G02 at 80m drives 6mm² selection).

decisions.label: 1.5/2.5/4/6mm² LSZH Cu cabling
decisions.summary: LSZH for low toxic emission in occupied plant room + genset enclosure; sizing matched to load + voltage-drop + cable-route environment.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Upstream MSB-MAIN F08 = 63A MCCB Type D on the utility-side; ATS sits between MSB-MAIN F08 and DB-GENSET-XCV; downstream MCBs at DB-GENSET-XCV are 6-32A Type C. Worst-case ratio at G02/G04 (32A) = 63:32 = ~2:1 — AT the IEC 60898 same-curve cascade threshold BUT benefits from MCCB-upstream-of-MCB cross-discrimination per IEC 60947-2 (Type D magnetic 10-20×In vs Type C 5-10×In typically gives reliable cascade at ratios ≥1.5:1). Verdict info — engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade chart; fault-level skill to confirm at BOTH 9.0 kA utility-mode and ~1.0 kA genset-mode subtransient Ik" (sustained Ik ~0.4 kA after AVR settles) + ATS transfer-time impact.

decisions.label: 63A:32A worst-case ratio = ~2:1 at G02/G04
decisions.summary: AT the IEC 60898 same-curve minimum; MCCB-class cross-discrimination via Type D upstream vs Type C downstream provides additional margin per IEC 60947-2.

decisions.label: Dual-mode cascade verification required
decisions.summary: Utility-mode PFC 9.0 kA + genset-mode subtransient Ik" ~1.0 kA (sustained Ik ~0.4 kA after AVR settles) — both regimes must be checked against manufacturer cascade table.

decisions.label: ATS transfer-time impact on cascade
decisions.summary: During the 6-10 s transfer window, the ATS is briefly in dwell (no source) — cascade behaviour cannot be analysed in steady-state alone; fault-level skill must model the transfer sequence.
decisions.rule: Time-domain cascade study for ATS-fed distribution

decisions.label: Defer to fault-level skill
decisions.summary: Manufacturer cascade table + ATS transfer-sequence behaviour + dual-mode PFC verification required at full coordination study.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: DB-GENSET-XCV is compliant against IEC 60364 series (general LV distribution), IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 (ATS + dual-source) + §551 (LV generating sets) + §556 (safety services), ISO 8528-12:1997 (genset emergency power supply), IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 (no-RCD on safety-services emergency feeds), IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4 (UPS bypass — applied to G04 RCD exemption), IEC 60364-4-41 §411.3.3 (Type A 30 mA on G05 mechanical-services socket-class circuit), and IEC 61439-3:2012 (distribution-board assembly). ONE WARNING flag: ATS transfer time (6-10 s) is AT NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 boundary and BELOW BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 Type Z threshold — AHJ sign-off required at commissioning.

decisions.label: G01 ATS controller RCD-free
decisions.summary: Dedicated ELV_control supervisory circuit — not a socket circuit; same exemption principle as DB-UPS U05 BMS controller.

decisions.label: G02 EM lighting feed RCD-free
decisions.summary: Life-safety emergency feed per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 — no RCD on safety-services distribution.

decisions.label: G03 fire alarm feed RCD-free
decisions.summary: Life-safety emergency feed per §560.7 — fire-alarm panel must be reachable for upstream fault current to clear.

decisions.label: G04 UPS bypass RCD-free
decisions.summary: UPS bypass mains-backup path per IEC 62040-1:2017 §5.2.4 — must allow upstream fault flow for proper isolation.

decisions.label: G05 day tank + cooling fan Type A 30 mA
decisions.summary: Mechanical-services 1-phase motor circuit — exterior-routed + fuel-handling + motor windings justify shock protection per §411.3.3.

decisions.label: WARNING flag — ATS transfer time AHJ disclosure
decisions.summary: 6-10 s typical sits AT NFPA 110-2022 Type 10 boundary and BELOW BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 §6.3 Type Z threshold; AHJ sign-off required at commissioning.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 5-row table with 3 spare ways (W6-W8) for future expansion. Spare-way reservation aligns with typical safety-services growth: additional CCTV/security panel feed, genset block heater for cold-climate retrofit, or genset-room utility lighting/socket. G01 (ELV control) + G02/G03/G04 (life-safety + UPS bypass) RCD-FREE; G05 (day tank + cooling fan) Type A 30 mA RCD. Placard 'DUAL-SOURCE — UTILITY/GENSET ATS — DO NOT BACK-FEED' on faceplate per IEEE 446-1995 + IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552.

decisions.label: 3 spare ways reserved
decisions.summary: Typical safety-services growth: CCTV/security feed, block heater for cold-climate retrofit, or genset-room utility lighting/socket.

decisions.label: Placard 'DUAL-SOURCE — DO NOT BACK-FEED'
decisions.summary: IEEE 446-1995 critical-power labelling + IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §552 dual-source warning requirement.

decisions.label: ATS transfer-time disclosure footer on schedule
decisions.summary: 6-10 s typical AHJ-disclosed; downstream DB-EM bridges via central battery; downstream DB-UPS bridges via 30-min UPS autonomy.
